# Restacked.ai

&gt; Context orchestration platform for AI-assisted development teams. This repository contains the V2 prototype codebase (iterating on V1 generated by v0) and a /context/ folder with project context files. Load context files at session start before generating any code or making any decisions.

## Always load first

- [DECISIONS.md](/context/DECISIONS.md): Architectural decisions already made -- never contradict these, flag conflicts explicitly

- [PROJECT.md](/context/PROJECT.md): Stack, home base, team roles, hard constraints, repo structure

- [ROLES.md](/context/ROLES.md): Two sections -- team roles (who builds) and product roles (who uses the product)

## Load for code tasks

- [GLOSSARY.md](/context/GLOSSARY.md): Exact domain vocabulary -- use these terms only, no synonyms

- [OPEN-QUESTIONS.md](/context/OPEN-QUESTIONS.md): Unresolved decisions -- stop and flag if your output touches any of these

## Load for discovery and planning tasks

- [DISCOVERY.md](/context/DISCOVERY.md): Project origin, pain points, reference tools, what we are not building

## Load when available (auto-generated, not yet active)

- SCHEMA.md: Data model from home base

- TYPES.md: TypeScript interfaces from home base

- API.md: Function signatures from home base

- CODEBASE-MAP.md: Module map -- load before any code generation to avoid duplicating existing logic

- CONVENTIONS.md: Detected code patterns -- load before any code generation to avoid contradicting existing patterns

## Rules that always apply

- Prototype code is never deployed to production -- always rewritten

- No direct database calls in UI -- abstraction layer only

- Never contradict DECISIONS.md -- flag conflicts explicitly and stop

- If output touches an item in OPEN-QUESTIONS.md -- stop and flag it

- Use exact terms from GLOSSARY.md -- no synonyms

- Role checks happen at the data layer, not just the UI

- Backend architecture decisions happen in the backend project only

- V2 is iterative on the V1 codebase -- load CODEBASE-MAP.md before generating new modules
